title: RA: Resident Advisor Magazine
description: News, features, reviews and podcasts from RA

items.title: Black Rave Culture announce new album
items.content_text: The DC trio will release Black Rave Culture Vol 4 on August 14th.

items.title: New 1,300-capacity club Fitz opens in Mallorca
items.content_text: The El Terreno space hosted a sold-out launch party with PAWSA and Nic Fanciulli last Thursday.

items.title: Ricardo Villalobos unveils first solo single of 2026
items.content_text: "Wings" is out on August 10th.

items.title: Standouts from 50 Years of Electronic Jazz
items.content_text: When jazz and electronic music combine, the possibilities are endless. Here are some of our favourite collaborations, remixes and breakthroughs across samba, techno, jungle, makossa, funk, free improv and beyond.

items.title: English clubs, pubs, venues to receive 20-percent business rate reduction
items.content_text: Andy Burnham, the UK's new Prime Minister and ex-Haçienda raver, announced the policy today.

items.title: Rusko steps away from music, citing mental health issues
items.content_text: The dubstep producer is on a "search for healing and recovery."

items.content_text: Back to the '80s.

items.title: Mix Of The Day: Yuoto Saito
items.content_text: Psychedelic dubstep and techno.

items.title: Sasha & John Digweed to reissue seminal Northern Exposure mix album
items.content_text: In celebration of the record's 30th anniversary, the forthcoming release includes new ambient and dub reworks.

items.title: SoundCloud acquires Nina Protocol
items.content_text: The streaming service will offer artist migration services and host the blockchain platform's editorial archive.

items.title: Porto's Plano B announces 20th anniversary celebrations
items.content_text: The programme features Ogazón, Objekt, Scúru Fitchádu and more.

items.title: Applications are open for Music Venue Trust's Liveline Fund
items.content_text: The fund was designed to support grassroots touring and create opportunities for artists in the UK.

items.title: London's Tottenham is getting a new music venue
items.content_text: The 250-capacity space is designed for club nights and live performances.

items.title: Mix Of The Day: Maher Daniel
items.content_text: A timeless festival set, recorded at Sunwaves in Bulgaria.

items.content_text: Two sublime hours of house and techno from the Berlin-based DJ and producer, making his long-awaited RA Mix debut.

items.content_text: Summery liquid and drum & bass.

items.title: Batu & Donato Dozzy - Exhale

items.title: Moscow festival Outline cancelled amid fears of Ukraine drone strikes
items.content_text: The event was set to take place from July 21st to 27th.

items.title: Miami's Medium Cool to close venue, announces search for new home
items.content_text: The Miami Beach bar is closing on August 22nd.

items.title: UK Music Chief Executive urges Andy Burnham to reduce costs of touring in Europe
items.content_text: The British organisation has set out five key tests for the new Prime Minister.

items.title: Slovenia's Festival Grounded to celebrate 10th anniversary with BADSISTA and Evanora Unlimited
items.content_text: The multi-venue event returns to Ljubljana on August 20th through 22nd.

items.title: AVA London announces 2026 Creators Forum
items.content_text: The ten-day development programme is open to artists aged 18 through 30.

items.title: Chicago's Signal Records expands to New York
items.content_text: The store will open a branch in Brooklyn later this year.

items.title: Spekki Webu - Bootstrap Paradox
items.content_text: Bending techno into thrilling new shapes, the Dutch artist conjures a trippy, alien album that speaks directly to rave culture's foundational spirit.

items.content_text: The 16-track record is out on July 31st via XL Recordings.

items.content_text: A patient, near-ten hour mix through an ever-shifting musical landscape.

items.title: Frankfurt's Module Festival confirms Suzanne Ciani, Skee Mask and upsammy for 2026 lineup
items.content_text: The free event will take over Kulturcampus Frankfurt on August 13th through 16th.

items.title: New experimental music club to open in Nijmegen
items.content_text: Stichting RBBR is transforming a former EKI rubber factory into a music venue.

items.title: Free open air events legalised in Berlin's Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg
items.content_text: The district becomes the first in the city granted permission to legally host spontaneous, non-commercial events outdoors.

items.content_text: Paul Kalkbrenner, Dixon, Fadi Mohem and Daria Kolosova will be heading to Greece this September.

items.title: Positive Education returns from hiatus with K Wata, ABADIR, Manuka Honey

items.title: This week's new music: Fundido, zayALLCAPS, bambinodj
items.content_text: Welcome to our weekly music round-up. Every Friday afternoon, we'll share ten announcements from the last few days.

items.title: The Cause adds Radio Alhara, Toumba, Kim Ann Foxman to festival lineup
items.content_text: The Cause FM: New Frequency will be held at the East London venue on July 25th.

items.title: fabric and Sennheiser to host Sven Väth at London's The Shard
items.content_text: The Farringdon club and audio company are collaborating on a series of live events and activations.

items.title: Korg releases NTS-4 six-channel performance mixer
items.content_text: The latest addition to the Nu:Tekt series ships as a solder-free DIY kit.

items.title: This week's top stories: RIP Lenzman, Boiler Room trampling, Divine Playhouse backlash
items.content_text: Five key electronic music stories from the last few days.

items.title: Various Artists - veneration for the sacred action
items.content_text: Blurring rap, poetry and jazz, the London collective's debut compilation captures a bracing scene built on proximity and improvisation.

items.title: Mix Of The Day: DJ Travella
items.content_text: Mix Of The Day: DJ Travella

items.title: Mix Of The Day: Azzi On The Beat & WEAREALLCHEMICALS
items.content_text: Get to know mara, the Nigerian subgenre of Afrobeats.

items.title: Arca announces takeover of worldwide radio stations
items.content_text: NTS, Kiosk Radio, Dublab and others will simultaneously air a mix of the powerhouse artist's latest project.

items.title: New York festival Sustain-Release reveals 2026 lineup
items.content_text: DJ Nobu, livwutang, Verraco, james K and more will play the beloved festival in the Catskills this September.

items.title: AIAIAI and Oklou unveil limited-edition headphone collaboration
items.content_text: Custom versions of the Danish audio brand's TMA-2 and Tracks headphones will arrive this month.

items.title: Listen back to three sets from AVA Belfast
items.content_text: DJ Gigola, Pegassi and Holly Lester are among the first batch of mixes released from the festival.

items.title: Tomorrowland fire reportedly ignited by ethanol leak
items.content_text: The fire completely destroyed the festival's main stage ahead of its opening.

items.title: Analogue Solutions releases limited edition monosynth
items.content_text: Filtopia is a fully analogue desktop instrument capable of creating unusual formant sounds.

items.title: New Manchester record shop opens in Northern Quarter
items.content_text: Onsite Records, located on 81 Newton Street, opened on July 5th.

items.title: New listening bar, Amber, opens in Vancouver
items.content_text: The music-first space is located inside the Anchor Building on 99 Powell Street, Gastown.

items.title: Ricardo Villalobos: Slipping Through Time
items.content_text: Irrepressible, mystical, human: who else does it like Ricardo Villalobos? In this rare interview, our second cover story of summer 2026, Oli Warwick goes deep with the electronic iconoclast.

items.title: Inside the Universe of When There Is No Sun
items.content_text: The union of Sun Ra and Ricardo Villalobos was box office enough. But the story behind 2026's cosmic compilation runs far deeper. Here, key figures including Chez Damier, Calibre, Barış K and Villalobos discuss the project's voyage from Istanbul to Saturn and back.

items.title: Sydney venue Divine Playhouse forced to close following backlash from Christian protesters
items.content_text: The queer arts space recently took over a former church that had spent 70 years as a theatre.

items.title: Mix Of The Day: Huey Mnemonic
items.content_text: Jumping Detroit funk.

items.title: Suno scraped songs from YouTube Music, Deezer, Genius, hacked data reveals
items.content_text: New information from a 2025 hack sheds light on where the text-to-music tool allegedly scraped songs from.

items.title: RA Pro Newsletter: Nina Protocol and the struggles of independence
items.content_text: Reflections on the recent closure of the beloved music platform.

items.title: San Francisco festival Entanglement announces lineup
items.content_text: The collaboration between local promoter Endezeit and Detroit's Interdimensional Transmissions will feature Suzanne Ciani, Wata Igarashi, Clarisa Kimskii and more.

items.title: Berlin club Kater launches reduced-price ticket for under-25s
items.content_text: The €15 Next-Gen Ticket is available to clubbers aged 18 to 24.

items.title: A new Actress album is on the way
items.content_text: Radical Frame is out on September 18th via Ninja Tune.

items.title: Logic1000 announces new album with Christine and the Queens, Jam City, FAUZIA
items.content_text: Confirmation! is out on October 2nd.

items.content_text: A lesson in universal love, told via deep club grooves.

items.title: Mix Of The Day: DJ Scott
items.content_text: Rapid-fire makina from 2004.

items.title: Change The Beat targets 300 label signings for women and gender-expansive artists by 2028
items.content_text: The nonprofit has also announced a new initiative, The Demo Room, connecting emerging artists directly with labels and A&Rs.

items.content_text: Listen to 65 tracks that the RA team heard on the ground at this year's festival.

items.title: Playlist: Draaimolen 2025
items.content_text: Listen to 66 tracks heard by RA staff at the Dutch festival last weekend.

items.title: Mix Of The Day: Ritornelo
items.content_text: A Bogotá staple shows off his range.

items.title: Playlist: Houghton 2025

items.title: Playlist: Horst Arts & Music 2025

items.title: Reptant - Ballet Robotique
items.content_text: Ice-cold 808s from electro's new prince.

items.title: Boards of Canada - Tape 05
items.content_text: Warp's mysterious duo are back after 13 years. The surprise: it's some of their most beautiful work yet.

items.title: fakemink - Terrified
items.content_text: After crossing over on a wave of hype, the self-proclaimed saviour of the UK underground wastes his moment on a bloated album of trap, self-aggrandisement and poorly mixed electronics.

items.title: Boards of Canada - Inferno
items.content_text: Warp's IDM icons resurface after 13 years with a dark, disquieting record shaped by a violent world. Say goodbye to the fuzz and wistfulness.

items.title: RHR - GÍRIA
items.content_text: Thrilling baile funk fusions from one of São Paulo's most compelling club whisperers.

items.title: Zora Jones - ANGEL CRISIS
items.content_text: Zora Jones's first solo project in five years ushers in a new era with transformative, high-drama bass music.

items.title: Excalibur and DJ Spence - Medium Rare
items.content_text: Fatigued by the fast pace of algorithmic slop? Reach for this EP of unhurried perfection from one of minimal's best labels.

items.title: Mala & Magugu - Militant Don
items.content_text: Mala goes back to basics with heavyweight results.

items.title: Beatrice M. - Midnight Swim
items.content_text: Another hit from France's best hope for a modern day dubstep great.

items.title: Protesters 'trampled' at Boiler Room New York
items.content_text: Video footage shows an attendee jumping onto protesters during a "die-in" at the Under the K Bridge event. Boiler Room said it respects "the right to peaceful protest."

items.title: Jasmín - Bite The Hand That Feeds You
items.content_text: Hessle Audio's 50th release reflects on the label's techno-dubstep roots while barrelling bass-first into the future.

items.title: Detroit festival Charivari unveils 2026 edition
items.content_text: DJ Bone, DJ Spen, Eddie Fowlkes, LADYMONIX and more will play across seven events at both TV Lounge and Tangent Gallery.

items.title: Munich club BLITZ unveils remaining July programme, closing party lineup
items.content_text: The marathon weekender will feature Seth Troxler, Sam Goku, Ogazón, DVS1, HTRK and dozens more.

items.title: Native Instruments announces collaboration with A.G. Cook
items.content_text: Super*Saw compiles A.G. Cook's signature stacked saw patches to a single synthesiser.

items.title: Answer Code Request - Halo
items.content_text: The Berlin producer picks up where his 2018 LP Gens left off, wringing extreme feeling from techno's austere templates.

items.title: New Pablo's Eye album to be released via STROOM
items.content_text: Everything you give away is out on August 16th.

items.title: Thaiboy Digital & swedm® - Paradise
items.content_text: The Drain Gang mainstay's latest LP takes on big-room trance with aplomb.

items.content_text: Gold Is The Metal (With The Broadest Shoulders), the first in the series, is out on October 30th.

items.content_text: Community Radio Index is an interactive online tool that maps community radio stations across the globe.

items.content_text: Divine Playhouse has opened in a former church that spent 70 years as a theatre.

items.title: PinkPantheress to make acting debut in new film from 'The Daniels'
items.content_text: The UK artist will join Matt Damon and Sandra Oh in the untitled Universal Pictures project.

items.content_text: Live at Twilo was recorded earlier this year at the revival of the legendary New York club.

items.title: Jeff Mills: Seeing Is Believing
items.content_text: What is techno? Is it a sound, style, way of life? And is it even in good health? In the first of our summer cover stories, Jeff Mills proposes a new answer to the age-old question.

items.title: London venue The Lion & Lamb cuts ties with cofounder Andre King amid abuse allegations

items.title: London club fabric announces autumn and winter programme
items.content_text: Ricardo Villalobos, Sama' Abdulhadi, Ogazón, tINI and dozens more will play between October and December.

items.title: This week's new music: Marina Herlop, FaltyDL, Agazero
items.content_text: We round up ten new music announcements from the last few days.

items.title: This week's top stories: Lukas Wigflex allegations, AlphaTheta unveils CDJ-1500X, Alex Jukes court hearing
items.content_text: Five key electronic music stories from the last few days.

items.title: Resident Advisor stage at Waterworks 2026 includes Eris Drew, OK Williams
items.content_text: Now taking place at East London venue The Cause, the festival has also announced set times.

items.title: Mix Of The Day: ENTROPÍA
items.content_text: Cumbia eclecticism.

items.title: Mix Of The Day: Sewa Choki
items.content_text: A comprehensive look at the Austin-based up-and-comer.

items.title: Lukas Wigflex cancels upcoming shows following allegations by ex-wife and business partner
items.content_text: Both parties shared new statements with Resident Advisor today. The Nottingham DJ has also stepped down from co-running UK festival Peep.

items.title: Djrum announces new EP, I Wander
items.content_text: The two-track release is out via Houndstooth on August 14th

items.title: A History of Ghettotech in Ten Tracks
items.content_text: Beloved by the likes of Zack Fox, MCR-T and SHERELLE, ghettotech is the ultimate floor-filler with its 808 architecture and attention-grabbing vocals. Learn about the quintessential Detroit sound via its pioneer, DJ Godfather.

items.title: Listen back to 12 sets from Finland's Solstice Festival
items.content_text: Mixes from Loidis, Kiernan Laveaux, livwutang and Verraco are available via The Lot Radio now.

items.title: AlphaTheta unveils compact CDJ-1500X multi player with cloud support and CoBeat integration
items.content_text: The new player borrows features from the flagship CDJ-3000X in a smaller format aimed at bars, small clubs and portable DJ setups.

items.title: Mix Of The Day: Dresden
items.content_text: A Glastonbury epic.

items.title: Jukebox PR founder Alex Jukes faces Crown Court hearing for stalking and assault allegations
items.content_text: The dance music podcaster and PR executive has pleaded not guilty to all charges.

items.title: Luca Durán's new album features Debit, Gavsborg, Rosa Pistola
items.content_text: Killing Mariposas is out on September 18th.

items.title: Village people cofounder Victor Willis dies aged 74
items.content_text: Willis died following a "short but aggressive illness," the group confirmed.
